steps.name: Evaluate @remotion/captions against scene-json-schema §3.6 constraints
steps.commit_sha: f11556d (decision recorded in code comment + here, no throwaway spike file needed)

steps.name: Custom segmentation algorithm (only if @remotion/captions doesn't fit)
Wire segments into SceneRenderer props preparation
Wire up tests + property test + verify all Acceptance Criteria
steps.commit_sha: f11556d (49/49 vitest green in packages/remotion-templates, incl. the mandatory no-text-lost property test)

decisions: Step 1: invoked /remotion-captions skill (mandatory per dev-guide.md §2.1) before writing any code. `createTikTokStyleCaptions()` groups tokens purely by a `combineTokensWithinMilliseconds` time window — it has no character-length limit and no linguistic concept of a Vietnamese number+unit cluster. A numeral and its following unit word ('92,5' + 'phần' + 'trăm') would only stay in the same caption page by coincidence of timing, not because the package understands BR-1's constraint. Concluded: doesn't fit, proceeded with a custom algorithm (Step 2) as the task's Scope anticipated. Skipped writing a disposable spike script since the mismatch is structural/documented in @remotion/captions' own API (time-window grouping only), not something that needed empirical disproof — recorded the reasoning directly in packages/remotion-templates/src/subtitle/segmentTimestamps.ts's module docstring instead of a throwaway file.

Segmentation implemented as a pure function (packages/remotion-templates/src/subtitle/segmentTimestamps.ts) operating on the canonical `{word, start_ms, end_ms}` shape from docs/specs/scene-json-schema.md §3.4 (voice.audio.timestamps) — not the raw internal edge_tts.py adapter shape (`{word, start, end}` in ticks), which is a separate, already-existing conversion boundary owned by task 2-4, not this task.

'Props preparation' (per the task's Step 4 file-location note) turned out to not need a separate backend service: no backend/app/services/scene_props.py or equivalent exists anywhere yet (checked — 2-2/2-3 never built one), and frontend's ScenePlayer.tsx already forwards the whole scene object as Remotion Player inputProps generically. So segmentation is computed inside SceneRenderer itself via useMemo(() => segmentTimestamps(...), [timestamps]) — this satisfies spec §3.4's 'generated at props-preparation time, not stored in Scene JSON' without inventing a new backend layer speculatively (rules/architecture.md: don't pre-split services before a measured need).

Subtitle.tsx's actual render decision (BR-3 enabled/disabled, active-segment lookup) was extracted into a pure exported helper `resolveSubtitleRenderState()` so it's unit-testable without a Remotion render/DOM context — this package's existing test suite (scene-renderer.test.ts) only tests pure logic, no React Testing Library or jsdom is set up, so this follows the established convention rather than adding new test infra for one component.

Found (not fixed, out of scope): frontend/vitest.config.ts imports '@vitejs/plugin-react' which isn't listed in frontend/package.json — `npm run test` in frontend/ fails at config-load time regardless of what's being tested. Pre-existing, unrelated to 2-5 (didn't touch vitest.config.ts or frontend deps) — flagged in memory/project-memory.md.
No docs/ contract change needed — consumed the already-documented voice.audio.timestamps/subtitle shape from scene-json-schema.md §3.4 as-is, added no new Scene JSON fields.
